ocr.text: In the context of the evolving hospitality landscape in the Asia-Pacific region, the analysis of Average Daily Rate (ADR) and
Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) trends is crucial for stakeholders aiming to optimize their market positioning. The data
presented reveals significant shifts from 2019 to 2020, particularly in the ADR metrics, where the average rate for hotels
increased to $160, reflecting broader market dynamics.

GrowthRate  MarketShare

[HIDDEN]  $145  3.5%  18%  [HIDDEN]
2021  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  $130
Notably, the total RevPAR for the market demonstrated a growth rate of 7.0%, underscoring a recovery trajectory amidst
fluctuating occupancy rates. It is also essential to highlight that the forecasted market share for resorts has been projected at
AUD200, while motels have shown an average ADR of 70% alongside a market share of AUD110. These figures illustrate a
competitive landscape, with resorts expected to achieve a growth forecast of 25%.

Furthermore, the overall occupancy rate increased to 22%, indicating a rebound in traveler confidence and demand across
various segments. As such, these insights into ADR and RevPAR trends will guide strategic decision-making for hospitality
operators and investors looking to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the region.
